groups.description: Purpose and permissible use of the Amateur Radio Service; Operator/primary station license grant;
Frequency allocations; Emission modes; Spectrum sharing; Transmissions near band edges; Contacting the
Licensing: classes, sequential and vanity call sign systems, places where the Amateur Radio Service is
Authorized and prohibited transmissions; Sale of equipment
Control operator: eligibility, designating, privileges, duties, location, required; Control point; Control types:
Station identification; Repeaters; Third party communications; Club stations; FCC inspection
Station operation: choosing an operating frequency, calling another station, test transmissions; Band plans:
VHF/UHF operating practices: FM repeater, simplex, reverse splits; Access tones: CTCSS, DTMF; DMR
Public service: emergency operations, applicability of FCC rules, RACES and ARES, net and traffic procedures,
Radio wave characteristics: how a radio signal travels, fading, multipath, polarization, wavelength vs
Electromagnetic wave properties: wavelength vs frequency, nature and velocity of electromagnetic waves,
Propagation modes: sporadic E, meteor scatter, auroral propagation, tropospheric ducting; F region skip;
Station setup: connecting a microphone, RF power meter, a power source, a computer, digital equipment,
Operating controls: frequency tuning, use of filters, squelch function, AGC, memory channels, noise blanker,
Current and voltage: terminology and units, conductors and insulators, alternating and direct current
Math for electronics: conversion of electrical units, decibels
Capacitance and inductance terminology and units; Radio frequency definition and units; Impedance
Ohm’s Law; Series and parallel circuits
Fixed and variable resistors; Capacitors; Inductors; Fuses; Switches; Batteries
Semiconductors: basic principles and applications of solid-state devices, diodes and transistors; Gain
Circuit diagrams: use of schematics, basic structure; Schematic symbols of basic components
Component functions: rectifiers, relays, voltage regulators, meters, indicators, integrated circuits,
Station equipment: receivers, transceivers, transmitter amplifiers, RF preamplifiers, transverters; Basic radio
Symptoms, causes, and cures of common transmitter and receiver problems: overload and overdrive,
Antenna and transmission line measurements and troubleshooting: measuring SWR, effects of high SWR,
Using basic test instruments: voltmeter, ammeter, and ohmmeter; Soldering
Basic characteristics of FM and SSB; Bandwidth of various modulation modes: CW, SSB, FM, fast-scan TV;
Amateur satellite operation: Doppler shift, basic orbits, operating protocols, modulation mode selection,
Operating activities: radio direction finding, contests, linking over the internet, exchanging grid locators
Non-voice and digital communications: image signals and definition of NTSC, CW, packet radio, PSK, APRS,
Antennas: vertical and horizontal polarization, concept of antenna gain, definition and types of beam
Feed lines: types, attenuation vs frequency, selecting; SWR concepts; Antenna tuners (couplers); RF
Power circuits and hazards: hazardous voltages, fuses and circuit breakers, grounding, electrical code
Antenna safety: tower safety and grounding, installing antennas, antenna supports
RF hazards: radiation exposure, proximity to antennas, recognized safe power levels, radiation types, duty
